evals.expected_output: Pushes SE Status to Salesforce — no deal context pull, just push
expectations: Checks sf CLI is installed and authenticated
Account file read from {config.company_folder}/Accounts/Acme Corp/Acme Corp.md
Opportunity ID extracted from salesforce_opportunity frontmatter field
No deal context fields queried (push mode only pushes)
Salesforce Updates code block content extracted
Content written to temp file /tmp/sf_update.txt
Access token obtained via sf org display

JSON payload built with HTML formatting (newlines to
, bold headers)
REST API PATCH to /services/data/v62.0/sobjects/Opportunity/{id} with {config.salesforce_se_status_field} field
Temp files cleaned up after update
Daily note checkbox for /sales-salesforce Acme Corp marked complete
Success report includes Opportunity ID and content preview

evals.expected_output: Reports error when account file doesn't exist
expectations: Error reported: account file not found
No REST API calls executed
No temp files created

evals.expected_output: Warns user when no opportunity URLs are in frontmatter
expectations: Account file read successfully
No salesforce_opportunity fields found or all empty
No push attempted
Warning message tells user to add salesforce_opportunity field

evals.expected_output: Imports all opportunities with historical deal context, creates files, builds Dataview table
expectations: Checks sf CLI is installed and authenticated
Mode detected as scan from 'scan' prefix in arguments
Account file read from {config.company_folder}/Accounts/Initech/Initech.md
Salesforce Account ID extracted from salesforce_account frontmatter field
Access token obtained via sf org display
SOQL queries executed for Opportunities, OpportunityFieldHistory, Events, and Notes
Raw query results saved to temp JSON file for subagent consumption
Subagents launched in parallel to process opportunity history (grouped by era: recent/older/historical)
Recent opportunities (last 12 months) get individual subagents with detailed milestone extraction
Older opportunities (1-3 years) batched into one subagent with key milestones only
Historical opportunities (3+ years) batched into one subagent with one-line summaries
opportunities/ subdirectory created under account folder
Individual opportunity .md files created with correct frontmatter
Duplicate opportunity names disambiguated with close date or ID suffix
opportunities.base file created with Dataview table configuration
Opportunity History section added to account file with ![[opportunities.base]] embed
Salesforce Deal History narrative added under Opportunity History with chronological milestones
Deal Ledger updated with Salesforce History section at bottom
Overall pattern analysis included (e.g., noting all closed-lost but existing customer)
salesforce_account field preserved in frontmatter
Temp files cleaned up
Summary table output with opportunity count, open/won/lost breakdown, and deal history summary

evals.expected_output: Falls back to extracting Account ID from an existing opportunity URL when salesforce_account is missing
expectations: Mode detected as scan
Account file read successfully
No salesforce_account field found in frontmatter
Falls back to salesforce_opportunity field to get an Opportunity ID
REST API call to /sobjects/Opportunity/{opp_id}?fields=AccountId to retrieve Account ID
Account ID used for SOQL queries
Proceeds with normal scan flow after obtaining Account ID

evals.expected_output: Asks user for Salesforce Account URL when no IDs can be determined
expectations: Mode detected as scan
Account file read successfully
No salesforce_account field found
No salesforce_opportunity fields found
Prompts user for Salesforce Account URL
No SOQL query executed without Account ID

evals.expected_output: Handles account with all closed opportunities correctly — notes discrepancy if account is an existing customer
expectations: All opportunities identified as closed (IsClosed = true)
Status correctly derived: Closed Won for IsWon=true, Closed Lost for IsWon=false
Opportunity files created for all closed opportunities
salesforce_opportunity fields left empty (no open opportunities to link)
Deal history notes that all opps are closed-lost but account appears to be existing customer
Summary reports 0 open opportunities

evals.expected_output: Multiple open opportunities are correctly linked in frontmatter with type-based suffixes
expectations: First open opportunity set as salesforce_opportunity
Additional open opportunities set as salesforce_opportunity_{type} (e.g., salesforce_opportunity_renewal)
Type suffix derived from Opportunity Type field, lowercased
Existing salesforce_opportunity fields for open opps preserved
Summary lists all open opportunities linked in frontmatter

evals.expected_output: Granularity scales with recency — recent opps get detailed timelines, old opps get one-liners
expectations: Opportunities grouped by era for subagent processing
Recent opportunity subagents extract detailed stage-by-stage timeline
Older opportunity subagents produce 2-4 bullet point summaries
Historical opportunity subagents produce single sentence summaries
Salesforce Deal History in account file organized by era with appropriate detail levels
Ledger Salesforce History section contains only meaningful milestones, not every activity

evals.expected_output: Disambiguates opportunity files when multiple opps share the same name
expectations: Duplicate names detected during file creation
First duplicate gets close date appended: Name (YYYY-MM-DD).md
If close dates also collide, falls back to ID suffix: Name (last8chars).md
All opportunity files have unique filenames
Frontmatter opportunity_name preserves the original Salesforce name

evals.expected_output: Pulls deal context from open opportunities, updates MEDDPICC and ledger, flags stale fields
expectations: Checks sf CLI is installed and authenticated
Mode detected as 'scan open' from prefix
Account file read successfully
Opportunity IDs extracted from salesforce_opportunity frontmatter fields
Deal context fields queried for each open opportunity (MEDDPICC, notes, stage history, ARR, forecast)
Stage history queried for each opportunity
Raw results saved to /tmp/sf_deal_context.json
Subagent launched to process deal context
Subagent reads account file and Ledger.md for cross-referencing
New MEDDPICC info from Salesforce appended with (from Salesforce) attribution
Ledger updated if deal has progressed since last entry
Stale fields flagged (stage, close date, ARR, forecast changes)
AE/CSM activity summarized — milestones included, routine outreach filtered out
Temp files cleaned up
Report shows per-opportunity summary with stage, amount, forecast, days in stage

evals.expected_output: Warns user to run scan first when no opportunity URLs exist
expectations: Mode detected as scan open
Account file read successfully
No salesforce_opportunity fields found or all empty
Warning tells user to run /sales-salesforce scan {Account} first
No SOQL queries executed

evals.expected_output: Filters noise from AE/CSM notes — includes milestones, excludes routine outreach
expectations: Deal milestones, pricing discussions, POV status included in context summary
Competitive dynamics and risk factors captured
Routine outreach attempts filtered out (emailed X no response, cadence activity)
Recent activity (last 30 days) more granular than older activity
Existing manually-written MEDDPICC content preserved, not overwritten
